reviews.review_description:
Doesn't seem right that they (you?) can just shut the app down with no regard to the money we've spent on concerts and songs.
  Not sure I would trust any app that may take it's place. 
Shame on you!

reviews.review_title: Ripped off and closed shop BEWARE!!!!
reviews.review_description: After purchasing several concerts and seeing that they CLAIMED to be making improvements on concerts and content etc... now they closed shop, stopped support and also shut down their website with no way to contact them.  Yup ripped us off and ran with the money. awesome.

reviews.review_title: Wtf the app don’t open now
reviews.review_description: All the concerts I paid for I can’t even open now the devs left the app and now u can’t open the app so now all paid concerts are gone this is bull crap a lot of people are gonna be pissed let’s sue this company 💯trash

Unfortunately I purchased a fair few videos and can not access any one of them!!

So lost money, especially disappointing as some of them I never got to watch as yet.

The video quality was appalling to be honest, yet it could have been a fantastic app.

So I guess I have been done!!
Could have been so much better

reviews.review_title: Has potential but not worth it right now
reviews.review_description:
I was very excited to try this app, however after purchasing the 02 Panic! Concert that I attended to relive memories of the night, I was disappointed to find the video quality was WAY below the standard that was expected.

As I live in the UK, super fast Internet speeds are not always available, and as such with no method of downloading what I have paid for to watch locally this is an issue. My average speed being 12MB. 

Additionally there is no settings offered to select the video quality manually and wait for buffering if you would prefer. There is no visual feedback to even show what your current video quality is!

Melody VR has so much potential but lacks user friendly features that are key for any app to be widely regarded as a success.
This app should come with a minimum Internet speed requirement or guide at least, as well as video quality settings even if it's just a 720p,1080p,1440p toggle or something/anything. For people with slower speeds local downloading should be an option for paid for in app purchased content.

Hopefully some of these features can be added in the future which would easily increase my rating.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review, we really appreciate the feedback! The app currently selects the best bitrate automatically to avoid buffering and keep the experience as immersive as possible. We can appreciate being able to select the quality could be handy - we'll be sure to pass this feedback onto the right team. In the meantime, you can keep an eye on any developments to the app on our blog at https://melodyvr.com/whats-new/

reviews.review_description: I don't understand the bad reviews!  Maybe you need a faster internet connection?  My experience has been nothing but stellar!  I have purchased 3 concerts for a FRACTION of what I paid to see them live, and I can watch it over and over with much better seats!!  Brendan Urie popping out of the floor right in front of me, and Dan Reynolds standing on the front rail at Red Rock where it seems I can almost touch him!!  Absolutely incredible!  Thanks MelodyVR!!!

developer_response.body: You're so welcome! Thanks for the review. Keep enjoying MelodyVR :)

reviews.review_title: I really think they have something here
reviews.review_description: I could easily see myself attending a live concert or even on-demand events using this platform; however, they badly need to find a way to improve the quality. I'm not quite sure what the issue is because a lot of VR video still looks pretty good (like YouTube 360 4K/8K/12K). The quality is just too low to enjoy any of the content at the moment but I'm really hoping this improves.

the videos are in 2d and not true 3d vr.

they should. look like the new 360 vr video elton john put out recently on youtube vr.

where the concert is in true 3D vr when he sings.

developer_response.body: We’re sorry you are unhappy with the app. We’re constantly looking to improve, so appreciate your feedback and will make sure we feed it in to future developments.

reviews.review_description: I only watched a bit of the Imagine Dragons event, so cannot speak for the others... but the quality is so poor that I found it absolutely unwatchable.  Great idea, I will gladly pay when they step up.. until then, this shouldn't be offered for free, much less $10

developer_response.body: We're sorry you feel this way. We’re constantly looking to improve, so appreciate your feedback. If there is something we can help you with, drop us an email at help@melodyvr.com and we'd  be happy to look in to it for you.

reviews.review_description: They have opted for the lesser immersive 2D 360° video format, which just looks flat and boring. There are some really good examples of 3D VR180 on YouTubeVR, which make you feel like you're actually there.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review and we appreciate the feedback! We’re constantly looking to improve the app - be sure to sign up to our newsletter so you can be the first to know about new shows and updates at https://melodyvr.com/sign-up/

reviews.review_description:
Great app on your phone and great on the quest too!! 
This is just the beginning of the journey !
Why not develop a monthly subscription or something?! 10 dollars for a show is expensive.

developer_response.body: We’re sorry you are unhappy with the app. We’re constantly looking to improve, so appreciate your feedback and will make sure we feed it in to future developments.

reviews.review_title: If You Just Want Good Seats .
reviews.review_description:
If you just want good seats the meh . However if you are an avid fan of live performance and VR then this is good stuff . The people who put this together really had done some homework . If you want  center floor seats for ten bucks then you deserve the veiw . It's primarily for the light shows , uhm Horray if you need hints on where to clap, sing or eh . Your real money makers are the stage views and the sound quality .   
   Transitioning from one scene to another is best done after viewing a show or set all the way through as they are not seamless ,if you purchase and entire performance which is your best bet  . 
If you're a fan of any of these artists why settle for being in the audience, unless you know for that song that's where you want to be whistles,bells and all ,but the feeling of being on stage and up close is something that these minor flaws are easily forgiven for . I'm sure I will watch these performances repeatedly like a live album to cherish in this technological age.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review! We’re glad you’re enjoying the app :)

The app is free to INSTALL but for each song or concert, you'll have to pay and he quality of the videos and sound is very poor most of the time. (and the artists barely interesting)
I love the app and stuff, but to do anything you gotta buy it
